# Adapting Pretrained Models: Prompting, PEFT, Quantization {#sec-17-peft-quantization}

::: {.callout-warning}
**Chapter stub.** This chapter is planned but not yet written. The abstract below is its
contract; drafting sources are listed for provenance.
:::

*When the model is too big to fine-tune, get surgical: prompting and in-context learning, LoRA's low-rank updates, quantization and QLoRA.*

<!-- draft-sources
seeds:
- `Module 11 slide decks (11.1-11.3)`
transcripts:
- `(slides-only module; no transcript)`
harvests:
- Chapter 9/15: transfer pays under the right scarcity/feature/coverage regime
- Chapter 15: full-backbone adaptation cost—fine-tuning changes every encoder
  weight; what remains when the backbone is too large to move?
- Chapter 16: "training-optimal is not serving-optimal"—compute-optimal
  pretraining does not make storage, inference, or adaptation affordable
-->
